sections.paragraph:
ἀφορμάω (s. ἀφορμή, ὁρμάω, ὁρμή: terms relating to motion; Hom. et al.; in act. Trag., X.+; TestSol 11:21 L for ἐφ-; Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 2 Jac.) to initiate movement from one point to another, start, set out εἰς τοὐπίσω start back again, return 1 Cl 25:4.—DELG s.v. ὄρνυμαι I; Frisk s.v. ὁρμή.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

act. act. = active

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Frisk Frisk = HF., Griechisches etymologisches Wörterbuch—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 158
